source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 6.5 Characters, words and lines (pp. 89-92)

questions.q: In command mode, delete the single character under the cursor.
questions.why: x deletes the character under the cursor; nx deletes n characters.

questions.q: What is the difference between r and R in command mode?
options: r replaces just one character; R enters replace mode to overwrite several (ended with Esc)
r replaces several characters; R replaces one
r deletes a character; R deletes a line
They both undo the last change
questions.why: r+character swaps a single character; R switches to replace mode (a kind of insert mode) until you press Esc.

questions.q: Delete (cut) the word starting under the cursor.
questions.why: dw deletes/cuts a word; position the cursor on the word's first character to cut the whole word.

questions.q: Copy (yank) the word starting under the cursor.
questions.why: yw copies a word into the buffer, ready to paste with p or P.

questions.q: After copying or cutting, which key pastes AFTER the cursor/line and which pastes BEFORE?
options: p pastes after; P pastes before
P pastes after; p pastes before
y pastes after; d pastes before
a pastes after; i pastes before
questions.why: Lowercase p pastes after the cursor (or current line); uppercase P pastes before it.
dd cuts the whole line; ndd cuts n lines.
yy copies the whole line; nyy copies n lines.

questions.q: Delete (cut) from the cursor to the END of the current line.
questions.why: d$ cuts from the cursor to the line end; d0 cuts from the start of the line to the cursor.

questions.q: Which command undoes the last action, and how do you redo (cancel the cancellation)?
options: u undoes the last action; Ctrl+R redoes it
U undoes the last action; u redoes it
questions.why: u undoes the last action (U undoes all changes on the current line); Ctrl+R redoes an undone action.
